blocks:
Ayahs

تَبَّتْ يَدَآ أَبِى لَهَبٍ وَتَبَّ (۱)
Perish the hands of Abu Lahab, and perish he! (1)
مَآ أَغْنَىٰ عَنْهُ مَالُهُۥ وَمَا كَسَبَ (۲)
Neither his wealth availed him, nor what he had earned. (2)
سَيَصْلَىٰ نَارًا ذَاتَ لَهَبٍ (۳)
Soon he will enter the blazing fire, (3)
وَٱمْرَأَتُهُۥ حَمَّالَةَ ٱلْحَطَبِ (٤)
and his wife [too], the firewood carrier, (4)
فِى جِيدِهَا حَبْلٌ مِّن مَّسَدٍۭ (٥)
with a rope of palm fibre around her neck. (5)

Key Points

This Surah is known by the names Tabbat and Masad. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Whenever you recite this Surah, curse Abu Lahab. (236)

When the Ayah, «و انذر عشیرتك الاقربین» (237), was revealed, the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله invited his close relatives and conveyed the message of revelation to them. Abu Lahab, who was the Prophet’s uncle, said: «تبّاً لك» (Woe unto you/Perish you). This Surah was revealed and turned his own words back against him. Since this curse was realized and Abu Lahab and his wife died in disbelief (kufr), it becomes clear that this Surah also contains a prophecy concerning Abu Lahab.

Tabbat (تبّت) is derived from the root tabb (تبّ), meaning loss and destruction. The intent of yad (ید) in the Ayah is not the physical hand, but rather a metaphor for the means of human power and effort. Therefore, the meaning of the Ayah is that Abu Lahab’s efforts were wasted, and he suffered loss and ruin. (238)

History records that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله invited people to the word «لااله الاّ اللّه» (There is no god but Allah), while Abu Lahab would follow Him, deny Him, and throw stones at Him. Sometimes pilgrims to Mecca would approach Abu Lahab and ask him about Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله. He would say: Muhammad is insane, and we are busy treating him. (239)

Abu Lahab never ceased his opposition to the Prophet and his cooperation with the Prophet’s opponents. He and his wife used vulgar language against the Prophet, and perhaps this is why their names are mentioned in the Quran. The tone of this Surah was so crushing that everyone memorized it. Abu Lahab suffered a heavy blow and became confined to his house until he died, and the remaining disbelievers took stock of their actions, fearing that a Surah might also be revealed against them.

Affections and family ties should not create a defect in faith and doctrine. «تبّت یدا ابى لهب»

Although Abu Lahab and his wife have died and their bones have decayed, these Ayahs must still be recited so that they serve as a lesson for others and increase the divine wrath and punishment upon them.

Abu Lahab had good brothers like Hamzah and Abu Talib, and a good father like Abdul Muttalib, but everyone has their own reckoning.

In the past, when bundles of firewood were collected, they were tied with a rope and carried on the shoulder. Since Abu Lahab’s wife used to scatter thorns (firewood) on the Prophet’s path to harm Him, the Quran states: On the Day of Judgment, she will also be the firewood for the Hellfire, and the ropes she used to collect the firewood will be hung around her neck. (240)

Abu Lahab did not have a son named Lahab, but perhaps he was called Abu Lahab (Father of Flame) because of the redness of his face.

Abu Lahab’s wife was a poet. Her name was Umm Jamil, the sister of Abu Sufyan and the aunt of Mu'awiyah. She held deep-seated malice toward the Prophet, scattered thorns on His path, and composed poems mocking and insulting Him.

On the Day of Judgment, wealth and possessions will be of no use to a person: «ما أغنى ماله و ماكسب» (His wealth and what he earned will not avail him). «ما اغنى عنّى مالیه» (My wealth has not availed me). «هلك عنّى سلطانیه» (241) (My authority has perished from me and was of no benefit to me). «ما اغنى عنكم جمعكم» (242) (Your gathering and wealth did not enrich you). «لن تغنى عنهم اموالهم و لا اولادهم» (243) (Neither their wealth nor their children will avail them).

Four types of couples are mentioned in the Quran: 1. A couple who are united and cooperative in good deeds. (Like Ali and Fatimah عليهما السلام, who fed the orphan, the captive, and the needy.) «و یطعمون الطعام» (244) 2. A couple who are united in evil deeds. «ابى لهب... و امراته» 3. A couple where the husband is on the path of truth and the wife is on the path of falsehood, such as Noah and Lot, whose wives did not believe in them and sided with the opponents. «كانتا تحت عبدین» (245) 4. A couple where the wife is on the path of truth and the husband is on the path of falsehood, such as Pharaoh and his wife. «ضرب اللّه مثلاً للذین آمنوا امرأت فرعون» (246)

Abu Lahab’s wife had a valuable necklace, which she swore to spend in opposition to the Prophet. «فى جیدها حبل من مسد»

Imam Ali عليه السلام wrote in a letter to Mu'awiyah: «منّا خیر نساء العالمین و منكم حمّالة الحطب» (247) (The best of the women of the worlds is from us, and the carrier of the firewood [for the fire] is from you).

Salman al-Farsi becomes part of the Prophet’s Household (Ahl al-Bayt), while Abu Lahab, the Prophet’s uncle, is subjected to the curse and damnation of Allah.

Since Abu Lahab’s wife scattered firewood (thorns) on the Prophet’s path, it is possible that «حمّالة الحطب» (the carrier of the firewood) refers to her entering Hell while carrying firewood on her shoulder.

The hand and arm are for effort, and effort is for reaching the desired goal. Abu Lahab’s goal was to prevent the growth of Islam, and He exerted great effort in this path, but he did not reach his goal. Thus, the effort of his arm was wasted. «تبّت یدا ابى لهب»

236) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq. 237) Surah Ash-Shu'ara, 214. 238) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 239) Tafsir Namūneh. 240) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 241) Surah Al-Haqqah, 29. 242) Surah Al-A'raf, 48. 243) Surah Aal-i Imran, 10. 244) Surah Al-Insan, 8. 245) Surah At-Tahrim, 10. 246) Surah At-Tahrim, 11. 247) Nahj al-Balaghah, Letter 28.

Messages

1- Declaring dissociation (bara’ah) from the unworthy is part of the religion. «تبّت یدا ابى لهب»

2- The punishment for insulting sacred things is eternal cursing and damnation. «تبّت یدا ابى لهب»

3- We must pray for the complete destruction of disbelief (kufr). «یدا» (The cutting off of the two hands is a sign of efforts being rendered fruitless.)

4- Respond to opponents in kind. In response to those who told the Prophet: «تبّاً لك» (Woe unto you), Allah said: «تبّت یدا ابى لهب»

5- In a doctrinal system, principle takes precedence over relationship. «تبّت یدا ابى لهب»

6- Wealth and possessions are among the tools used by the opponents of the prophets. «ما اغنى عنه ماله و ما كسب» (Abu Lahab was a capitalist who spent his wealth to erase Islam.)

7- Worse than corruption is its repetition. «حمّالة»

8- Against divine wrath, neither wealth nor anything gained through effort—whether it be wealth, status, title, government, or fame—is effective. «ما اغنى عنه ماله و ما كسب»

9- Divine wrath against oppressors is not far off. «سیصلى»

10- Worldly aristocracy is accompanied by humiliation in the Hereafter. «فى جیدها حبل من مسد»
